intro: Pick the situation that sounds like yours.

services.title: Start a new project
services.hook: Turn an idea into something real people can evaluate.
fits: You have an idea and want to test market fit.
You want a working demo to share with selected users.
You need something credible to present to investors.

examples.name: BlockByBlock
examples.idea: An early-stage product that needed investor proof before committing to a larger build.
examples.approach: Cut scope to the one flow investors had to see, built a realistic full-stack demo on GCP, and kept MVP shortcuts separated from foundations meant to survive.
examples.outcome: Investor-ready demo in 10 days, ~$10k of premature build avoided, architecture sized for a ~4,000-person waitlist.

examples.name: CommunityPulse
examples.idea: A tool to surface how a community actually feels about decisions before they ship.
examples.approach: Started from the smallest loop that produces a real signal — collect, aggregate, show — and validated with a selected group before widening scope.
examples.outcome: A working prototype used to test the concept with early users.

examples.name: Bugdasht
examples.idea: A bug-bounty platform connecting companies with security researchers.
examples.approach: Led the build from initiation to market readiness with a security-first architecture, cryptographic data protection, and external researchers stress-testing the platform.
examples.outcome: MVP live in under 8 months; a critical business-flow security flaw was found and fixed before it could cost money.

examples.name: DTip
examples.idea: Transparent, non-custodial donations that work across blockchains.
examples.approach: Shipped an MVP integrating smart contracts with a web frontend, designed the donation UX for non-technical donors, and documented it for contributors.
examples.outcome: Live open-source dApp with real-time on-chain visibility of every donation.

examples.name: DCPay
examples.idea: A trustless payment gateway operated by autonomous AI agents.
examples.approach: Built the smallest end-to-end agent workflow (Python, OLAS) that could prove the trustless payment concept.
examples.outcome: Working demo demonstrating the concept without a trusted middleman.

services.title: Get ready for real users
services.hook: Your prototype works — make it safe to launch and operate.
fits: The demo works, but real users are about to arrive.
Failures are silent and releases feel like a gamble.
You need security, monitoring, and a sane deploy path.

examples.name: Preparing an AI Product for Real Users

services.title: Scale without scaling cost
services.hook: Handle growth without buying more servers by default.
fits: Traffic is growing and reliability is dropping.
Infrastructure spend is eating the runway.
Adding hardware stopped fixing the problem.

examples.name: Scaling a Realtime Platform Without Higher Hosting Costs
Reducing AWS Costs by 60% Without Slowing the Product

services.title: Automate repetitive work
services.hook: Give skilled people their time back — without hiding failures.
fits: Experts spend hours a day on manual, repeatable checks.
Manual repetition is causing inconsistency and errors.
You want automation that stays auditable and stoppable.

examples.name: Automating Three Hours of Daily Security Operations
